title: DIY Angled Coffee Table
summary: Learn how to build this DIY Coffee table for less than $40 in lumber!
description: Learn how to build this DIY Coffee table for less than $40 in lumber! You only need 8 boards and 3 tools! Free plans by Shanty2Chic!

steps.title: Step 1
steps.body: Cut the table top planks and drill 3/4" pocket holes, as shown on the cut list. Attach the planks together with 1 1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.body: Measure and cut the breadboards to size. Attach them to the ends of the planks with 1 1/4" pocket hole screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.body: Cut the base pieces to size and drill 1 1/2" pocket holes into the angled ends of each foot. Attach the feet to the runner with 2 1/2" pocket hole screws and wood glue.Build two of these bases.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.body: Cut the legs to size and attach them to the top base with 2 1/2" wood screws from the top of the base and into the legs. The pocket holes should face up.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.body: Flip the base over and attach the bottom base with wood glue and 2 1/2" wood screws from the bottom of the bottom base and into the legs. The pocket holes should face down.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.body: With the table top upside down, center the base on top of it. Attach the table base to the top with 2 1/2" wood screws from the top of the base and into the table top.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.body: Now it's time to sand the table down and add a finish! Make sure to share your builds with us on social media and use #shanty2chic so we can share them too!
